name: ACCESS Florida / SNAP (Food Assistance)
description: Florida's Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) provides monthly food assistance benefits to low-income individuals and families via EBT card through the ACCESS Florida system.
eligibilitySummary: Florida residents who are U.S. citizens or qualified non-citizens with low income. Work requirements apply for certain individuals aged 16-64.
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level for most households (BBCE). No asset limit in Florida.
hours: MyACCESS portal available 24/7; Customer Call Center hours vary

name: MyACCESS Florida Self-Service Portal
description: Online self-service portal for applying to and managing public assistance benefits including SNAP, Medicaid, and Temporary Cash Assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Any Florida resident seeking to apply for or manage public assistance benefits.
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: Florida Medicaid
description: Florida's Medicaid program provides free or low-cost health coverage to eligible low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly adults, and people with disabilities.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, parents/caretakers, elderly, and disabled Florida residents. Florida has not expanded Medicaid under the ACA.
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by category. Children up to 200-215% FPL. Pregnant women up to 191% FPL. Parents/caretakers approximately 28% FPL. SSI recipients automatically eligible.

name: Florida KidCare
description: Florida's children's health insurance program offering high-quality, income-based health insurance for children from birth through age 18, including Medicaid for children, MediKids, Florida Healthy Kids, and CMS.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under age 19 who are Florida residents and U.S. citizens or qualified non-citizens.
incomeLimitNotes: Free for children in families at or below 200% FPL; sliding-scale premiums up to $15-20/month for families up to 400% FPL.

name: Florida Reemployment Assistance (Unemployment Insurance)
description: Florida's unemployment insurance program providing temporary wage replacement benefits to eligible workers who are unemployed through no fault of their own.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own, are able and available to work, and are actively seeking employment.
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned at least $3,400 during the base period with wages in at least two quarters. Weekly benefits range from $32 to $275.

name: CareerSource Florida
description: Florida's statewide workforce development board connecting job seekers with employment services, training programs, career counseling, and employer connections through a network of local career centers.
eligibilitySummary: All Florida job seekers and employers. Priority services for veterans, individuals with disabilities, and low-income individuals.

name: CareerSource South Florida
description: Regional workforce development board serving Miami-Dade and Monroe counties with job placement, training, career counseling, and support services.
eligibilitySummary: Job seekers and employers in Miami-Dade and Monroe counties.

name: Florida Housing Finance Corporation
description: State agency providing affordable housing opportunities through rental assistance, homebuyer programs, SHIP grants, and the SAIL loan program for developers of affordable housing.
address: 227 North Bronough Street, Suite 5000, Tallahassee, FL 32301
eligibilitySummary: Low-income and moderate-income Florida residents seeking affordable rental or homeownership opportunities.
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by program and county. Generally targets households at or below 80% of Area Median Income.

name: SHIP - State Housing Initiatives Partnership Program
description: Provides funds to all 67 Florida counties and larger cities for down payment assistance, home rehabilitation, emergency repairs, and affordable housing development for low-income residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income and very low-income Florida residents. Contact your county's SHIP administrator to apply.
incomeLimitNotes: Generally serves households with incomes at or below 120% of Area Median Income, with priority for very low-income households.

name: Florida Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
description: Federally funded program providing utility payment assistance to low-income households to help meet heating and cooling costs and prevent service disconnection.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Florida households, with priority for households with children under 5, elderly members, or disabled individuals.
incomeLimitNotes: Total household income must be no more than 60% of the Florida State Median Income. Crisis assistance available year-round.
hours: Heating: October 1 - March 31; Cooling: April 1 - September 30; Crisis: year-round

name: Florida Legal Services
description: Statewide nonprofit providing free civil legal assistance to low-income Floridians including veterans, immigrants, tenants, domestic violence survivors, and individuals facing consumer issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Florida residents facing civil legal issues including housing, immigration, consumer rights, and domestic violence.
incomeLimitNotes: Generally serves individuals at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level.

name: Florida Law Help
description: Online legal resource portal helping Floridians find free legal aid providers by location or topic, with self-help resources and legal information.
eligibilitySummary: Any Florida resident seeking free legal assistance or information.

name: Legal Services of Greater Miami
description: Provides free civil legal services for low-income communities in Miami-Dade and Monroe counties, covering housing, family law, immigration, public benefits, and consumer protection.
address: 4343 West Flagler Street, Suite 100, Miami, FL 33134
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of Miami-Dade and Monroe counties facing civil legal issues.
incomeLimitNotes: Generally serves individuals at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level.

name: 211 Florida
description: Free, confidential 24/7 helpline connecting Floridians with local social services including food, housing, utilities assistance, health care, transportation, child care, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: Any Florida resident seeking information about or referrals to social services and community resources.
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter services

name: Feeding Florida
description: Statewide food bank network coordinating 9 Feeding America-affiliated regional food banks to fight hunger across all 67 Florida counties through food distribution and advocacy.
eligibilitySummary: Any Florida resident experiencing food insecurity. No documentation required at most distribution sites.

name: Feeding South Florida
description: Feeding America-affiliated food bank serving Palm Beach, Broward, Miami-Dade, and Monroe counties with food distribution through over 300 partner agencies.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of Palm Beach, Broward, Miami-Dade, and Monroe counties experiencing food insecurity.
hours: Varies by distribution site and partner agency

name: Second Harvest Food Bank of Central Florida
description: Feeding America-affiliated food bank serving seven Central Florida counties (Brevard, Lake, Marion, Orange, Osceola, Seminole, Volusia) through food distribution and hunger relief programs.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of Brevard, Lake, Marion, Orange, Osceola, Seminole, and Volusia counties experiencing food insecurity.

name: Feeding Northeast Florida
description: Feeding America-affiliated food bank serving 12 Northeast Florida counties through over 400 partner agencies and food programs.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of Alachua, Baker, Bradford, Clay, Duval, Flagler, Gilchrist, Levy, Nassau, Putnam, St. Johns, and Union counties experiencing food insecurity.

name: Harry Chapin Food Bank of Southwest Florida
description: Feeding America-affiliated food bank serving Charlotte, Collier, DeSoto, Glades, Hendry, and Lee counties through food distribution and hunger relief programs.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of Charlotte, Collier, DeSoto, Glades, Hendry, and Lee counties experiencing food insecurity.

name: Florida Department of Veterans' Affairs (FDVA)
description: State agency connecting Florida's 1.5 million veterans with federal and state benefits, healthcare, education, employment, and support services including state veterans' nursing homes and domiciliary homes.
eligibilitySummary: Florida veterans and their eligible dependents and survivors.

name: FDVA Benefits & Services
description: Comprehensive benefits assistance for Florida veterans including claims filing, healthcare enrollment, education benefits, employment services, and housing assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Florida veterans, active duty service members, and their families.
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by specific benefit program

name: Florida Temporary Cash Assistance (TCA / TANF)
description: Provides temporary cash assistance to families with children to help meet basic needs while transitioning to self-sufficiency, administered through the DCF Economic Self-Sufficiency program.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children under 18 (or under 19 if full-time high school students) who meet technical, income, and asset requirements.
incomeLimitNotes: Income and asset limits vary by family size. Must participate in work activities. Time-limited to 48 months.
hours: MyACCESS portal available 24/7; Call Center: Monday-Friday

name: Florida WIC (Women, Infants, and Children)
description: Federally funded nutrition program providing healthy foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals to pregnant women, new mothers, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant or breastfeeding women, infants, and children under age 5 who are Florida residents and meet income guidelines.
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level. Participants in Medicaid, SNAP, or TANF are automatically income-eligible.

name: Florida School Readiness Program (Child Care Subsidy)
description: State child care subsidy program offering financial assistance for early education and care to eligible low-income families so parents can work or attend school, administered through local Early Learning Coalitions.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families, families in education/training, and families receiving TCA, transitioning from TCA, or at risk of needing TCA. Children must be under age 13 (or under 22 with special needs).
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 150% of the Federal Poverty Level to enter the program. Families can remain eligible up to 85% of State Median Income.
hours: Varies by local Early Learning Coalition

name: Early Learning Coalition Family Portal
description: Online portal for Florida families to apply for School Readiness child care subsidies and manage their early learning services.
eligibilitySummary: Florida families seeking child care financial assistance through the School Readiness program.
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 150% FPL for initial eligibility.

name: Florida Healthy Start
description: Statewide program offering prenatal and infant screening, home visiting, care coordination, and support services to reduce infant mortality and improve health outcomes for pregnant women and children up to age 3.
eligibilitySummary: All pregnant women in Florida are offered screening; high-risk pregnant women, postpartum women, and their infants/children up to age 3 are eligible for services.
incomeLimitNotes: No income requirements. All high-risk pregnant women and infants are eligible regardless of income.
hours: Varies by local Healthy Start Coalition

name: Florida Commission for the Transportation Disadvantaged
description: State commission ensuring availability of transportation services for disabled, low-income, homeless, and at-risk individuals through coordinated community transportation in all 67 Florida counties.
eligibilitySummary: Disabled individuals, persons with income at or below 225% FPL, homeless individuals, adults and children at risk, and unemployed persons needing job training.
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 225% of the Federal Poverty Guidelines for income-based eligibility.

name: Miami-Dade Transportation Disadvantaged Program
description: Provides reduced-cost door-to-door transportation and discounted bus passes for transportation disadvantaged residents of Miami-Dade County.
eligibilitySummary: Miami-Dade County residents who are disabled, low-income, homeless, at-risk, or unemployed and needing job training.
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 225% of the Federal Poverty Guidelines.

name: Miami-Dade Community Action and Human Services Department
description: County department providing comprehensive social services including Head Start, elderly services, emergency food/shelter/utility assistance, home repair, domestic violence services, and employment programs.
eligibilitySummary: Miami-Dade County residents, primarily low-income families and individuals, seeking social services and community support.
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; many services target families at or below 125-200% FPL.

name: Florida DCF Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide trilingual hotline providing crisis support, safety planning, information, and referrals to local certified domestic violence centers for victims of domestic violence.
eligibilitySummary: Any person in Florida experiencing or at risk of domestic violence, regardless of income or immigration status.
hours: 24 hours a day, 365 days a year

name: Florida Abuse Hotline
description: Central reporting center for allegations of abuse, neglect, abandonment, and exploitation of children and vulnerable adults in Florida.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone can call to report suspected abuse, neglect, or exploitation of children or vulnerable adults.
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: Florida DCF Homelessness Programs
description: State Office on Homelessness overseeing policy, funding, and local Continuum of Care entities providing emergency shelter, outreach, rental assistance, and rapid rehousing services.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of homelessness in Florida.
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program. Emergency shelter has no income requirement. Rapid rehousing and prevention programs generally target households at or below 30% of Area Median Income.
hours: Varies by local Continuum of Care provider

name: Florida Division of Emergency Management
description: State agency coordinating disaster preparedness, response, and recovery including individual assistance, public assistance grants, and the State Assistance Information Line.
eligibilitySummary: Florida residents affected by declared disasters and emergencies.
hours: State Assistance Information Line activated during emergencies; office: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM ET

name: Florida Department of Elder Affairs
description: State agency serving Florida's elderly population through Area Agencies on Aging, providing information, services, and advocacy including the Elder Helpline, home and community-based services, and caregiver support.
eligibilitySummary: Florida residents aged 60 and older. Some programs serve adults 18+ with disabilities.
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program. Many services are available regardless of income, though some prioritize low-income elders.
hours: Elder Helpline: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM ET

name: Florida Bright Futures Scholarship Program
description: Lottery-funded scholarship program rewarding Florida high school graduates for academic achievement with tuition coverage at Florida public colleges and universities.
eligibilitySummary: Florida high school graduates who meet specific academic achievement criteria including GPA, test scores, and community service hours.
incomeLimitNotes: No income limit. Based on academic merit. Florida Academic Scholars receive 100% tuition; Medallion Scholars receive 75% tuition.

name: Covering Florida (ACA Navigator Program)
description: Federally funded navigator program helping Floridians understand their health insurance options and enroll in Marketplace coverage through HealthCare.gov.
eligibilitySummary: Any Florida resident seeking to enroll in health insurance coverage through the federal Marketplace.
incomeLimitNotes: Premium tax credits available for households between 100-400% FPL. Cost-sharing reductions for households below 250% FPL.
hours: Varies by navigator location; peak hours during Open Enrollment (November-January)

name: Florida Association of Community Health Centers
description: Statewide association supporting Federally Qualified Health Centers (FQHCs) that provide primary care, dental, behavioral health, and pharmacy services on a sliding fee scale regardless of ability to pay.
eligibilitySummary: All Florida residents can access FQHC services regardless of insurance status or ability to pay. Sliding fee scale based on income.
incomeLimitNotes: Sliding fee discounts available for patients with incomes at or below 200% FPL. No one is turned away for inability to pay.
languages: English, Spanish, and varies by location

name: Community Health of South Florida (CHI)
description: Network of community health centers in Miami-Dade and Monroe counties offering primary care, dental, behavioral health, and pharmacy services with sliding fee scale for uninsured patients.
eligibilitySummary: All residents of Miami-Dade and Monroe counties. Accepts most insurance and offers sliding fee scale for uninsured patients.
incomeLimitNotes: Sliding fee scale based on family income and household size for uninsured patients.

name: Florida Rural Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to qualifying low-income individuals in 13 Florida counties covering areas including housing, family law, public benefits, and consumer issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents in 13 rural Florida counties including DeSoto, Glades, Hardee, Hendry, Highlands, Indian River, Martin, Okeechobee, Osceola, Polk, and St. Lucie counties.
incomeLimitNotes: Generally serves individuals at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level.
hours: Monday-Friday, office hours vary by location

name: You Thrive Florida (Community Action Agency)
description: Nonprofit community action agency providing comprehensive services including energy/rent/mortgage assistance, weatherization, food distribution, early learning, senior services, affordable housing, and financial literacy.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in Florida seeking assistance with basic needs, energy costs, housing, or other supportive services.
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program. LIHEAP services require income at or below 60% of State Median Income. Other programs have different thresholds.
hours: Monday-Friday, office hours vary by location

name: Funding Florida Legal Aid (FFLA)
description: Statewide public charity that funds legal services organizations, develops innovative tools and programs, and supports legal aid providers and the courts serving low-income Floridians.
eligibilitySummary: Supports legal aid organizations serving low-income Florida residents.

name: Florida Disaster Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (D-SNAP)
description: Emergency food assistance program activated after federally declared disasters, providing one-time food benefits to disaster-affected households who do not normally receive SNAP.
eligibilitySummary: Florida residents in declared disaster areas who suffered food loss or damage, or who have disaster-related expenses. Must not be currently receiving SNAP.
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits are higher than regular SNAP during disaster periods. Disaster-related expenses are deducted from income for eligibility.
hours: Activated only during federally declared disasters

name: Miami-Dade County Light Bill Assistance (LIHEAP)
description: County-administered LIHEAP program helping low-income Miami-Dade residents pay electric and gas bills to prevent utility disconnection.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Miami-Dade County residents facing utility disconnection or high energy costs.
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 60% of the Florida State Median Income.

name: FloridaJobs.org (Department of Economic Opportunity)
description: Official state employment portal providing access to job listings, reemployment assistance, workforce programs, community development resources, and labor market information.
eligibilitySummary: All Florida job seekers and employers.
